Needle is placed through the marks on the Penrose drain.

frame_analyses.description: First throw of the surgeon's knot being executed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Surgeon's knot appears secure, using right hand.

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch properly executed, knot appears tight.

frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second hand switch properly executed, knot seems secure.
Both suture ends are cut successfully, completing the task.

knot_assessments.notes: Surgeon's knot is successful and secure.
Second throw completed with hand switch.
Third throw completed with hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No gap visible, drain remains intact.

technique_summary: The task was completed with slight deviations in suture placement. Knots were securely tied, with proper hand switching observed after the first throw.
improvement_suggestions: Work on reducing suture deviation for better accuracy.
Maintain steady pace to improve completion time.
strengths: Secure knot tying with effective hand switching.
No gap visible in the Penrose slit.
